title: 8. I Love America | Do We Get to Win This Time?
format: The Vietnam-on-film series closes out the boom: Born on the Fourth of July, Running on Empty and Dead Presidents move the war home, then the Gulf War and Saving Private Ryan send Hollywood back to WWII.

segments.heading: How Hollywood Made the Vietnam War
segments.note: The end of the boom, from Born on the Fourth of July to Dead Presidents

films.title: Sands of Iwo Jima
films.note: The John Wayne WWII film Ron Kovic watched as a Long Island kid; Wayne's Sgt. Stryker death scene fed Kovic's love of the military.

films.title: The Green Berets
films.note: Wayne's pro-war Vietnam picture, which a wounded Kovic saw on a hospital pass and rejected: 'This is not the way it was.'

films.title: Coming Home
films.note: Jane Fonda's Oscar winner, which Bruce Gilbert says Kovic's speeches helped inspire ('I lost my body to regain my mind').

films.title: Born on the Fourth of July
films.note: Oliver Stone's adaptation of Kovic's memoir, a decade in the making after an Al Pacino version collapsed. Tom Cruise took a pay cut and used a wheelchair for months; won Stone a second directing Oscar and made $70M.

films.title: The Exorcist
films.note: Cited for William Friedkin, first attached to direct the aborted late-70s Born on the Fourth of July.

films.title: The French Connection
films.note: Friedkin's other 70s landmark, named alongside The Exorcist.

films.title: Running on Empty
films.note: Naomi Foner's script about ex-radicals underground since bombing a napalm lab; Sidney Lumet directed River Phoenix, Judd Hirsch and Christine Lahti. Golden Globe for Foner, Oscar nods for her and Phoenix.

films.title: 12 Angry Men
films.note: Named as a Lumet classic.

films.title: Dog Day Afternoon
films.note: Named as a Lumet classic.

films.title: Midnight Express
films.note: Stone's screenwriting Oscar, part of the recap of what he did while Born on the Fourth of July stalled.

films.title: Salvador
films.note: Stone's film that, per the narrator, gave him a 'James Woods-sized headache.'

films.title: Platoon
films.note: Stone's breakthrough; Le Ly Hayslip handed out flyers outside her San Diego theater asking about Vietnamese dead, and it helped clear the way for Heaven and Earth.

films.title: Wall Street
films.note: Stone's 'greed is good' hit, his other post-Platoon success.

films.title: The Deer Hunter
films.note: Cited alongside Coming Home as the earlier wave of tragedy-driven Vietnam movies.

films.title: The Exterminator
films.note: Example of the 80s damaged-vet action drama that treated Vietnam vets as ticking time bombs.

films.title: The Park Is Mine
films.note: Named in the same breath as The Exterminator: a vet who no longer fits into society and pushes back violently.

films.title: Risky Business
films.note: Cruise's breakout; the episode's own correction notes it came out in 1983, not 1982.

films.title: Top Gun
films.note: The jingoistic Navy-pilot blockbuster that made Stone wary of casting Cruise as Kovic.

films.title: Dogfight
films.note: Nancy Savoca's gentle 1963-set romance; River Phoenix as Marine Birdlace, Lili Taylor as Rose. Savoca cut it as the Gulf War began and feared history repeating.

films.title: The Doors
films.note: Stone's rock biopic; Hayslip made him promise Heaven and Earth would have none of its sex.

films.title: Heaven and Earth
films.note: The last film of Stone's Vietnam trilogy, from Le Ly Hayslip's memoir; Hiep Thi Le leads opposite Tommy Lee Jones. A $23M epic that audiences and critics ignored ('I got slaughtered').

films.title: Forrest Gump
films.note: Robert Zemeckis's six-Oscar hit; Tom Hanks as Gump, Gary Sinise as Lieutenant Dan, whose arc echoes Kovic's before ending happy.

films.title: Menace II Society
films.note: Albert and Allen Hughes's surprise-hit LA crime debut, made in their early 20s.

films.title: Dead Presidents
films.note: The Hughes brothers' bleak Bronx-vet heist drama, inspired by Wallace Terry's oral history Bloods; Larenz Tate as Anthony, Chris Tucker, Keith David, Freddy Rodriguez, Martin Sheen as the judge. The boom's explosive finale.

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: The poster on the Hughes brothers' studio wall; Martin Sheen's cameo as the sentencing judge nods to his lead role here.

films.title: Saving Private Ryan
films.note: Spielberg's D-Day film that the narrator watched with two WWII vets; near half a billion worldwide, it spearheaded Hollywood's WWII revival.

films.title: The Longest Day
films.note: The John Wayne WWII hit one of those vets had been an extra in.

films.title: The Thin Red Line
films.note: Terrence Malick's Pacific-theater drama, part of the WWII revival Ryan set off.

films.title: Pearl Harbor
films.note: Named among the big-budget, A-list WWII films that followed Saving Private Ryan.

films.title: Windtalkers
films.note: Named among the WWII revival films.

films.title: Hart's War
films.note: Named among the WWII revival films.

films.title: For the Boys
films.note: One of the pricey 90s Vietnam dramas that underperformed as audiences lost interest.

films.title: The War
films.note: Another pricey Vietnam-era drama that underperformed.

films.title: Flight of the Intruder
films.note: A Vietnam drama that downright bombed.

films.title: The Fog of War
films.note: Cited as a tough-minded Vietnam documentary from the last 20 years.

films.title: Tropic Thunder
films.note: Cited as a later Vietnam-adjacent dark comedy.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Spike Lee's later Vietnam drama, named as a rare recent return to the war.

films.title: The Hurt Locker
films.note: Named among the post-9/11 war hits, contrasted with waning interest in Vietnam.

films.title: American Sniper
films.note: Named among the post-9/11 war hits.

films.title: Lone Survivor
films.note: Named among the post-9/11 war hits.

films.title: Zero Dark Thirty
films.note: Named among the post-9/11 war hits.

films.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
films.note: The WWII-homecoming film Cue Chin's father translated for her in a Hanoi movie palace; it closes the series.

Books: When Heaven and Earth Changed Places
Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition

Born on the Fourth of July.title: Born on the Fourth of July

The Best Years of Our Lives.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
